summary: Excellent matrix recording of a legendary late‑tour show with crisp sound and standout moments like Brent’s “I Will Take You Home” and a fiery “Hell in a Bucket.”
review: The matrix tape captures the band at a high point – clear, punchy mix with no mud or Dolby, letting every instrument shine.  Performances are praised for their energy, especially Brent’s vocal on “I Will Take You Home,” the soaring “Hell in a Bucket,” and a tight set that rolls from Shakedown Street through Sugaree and Estimated Prophet into an expansive post‑Space jam.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (4–5) and enthusiastic comments about both the musical performance and the pristine matrix sound push the rating into the strong‑plus range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with minimal mud; no Dolby compression; praised as one of the best‑sounding archive recordings.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid opener with Shakedown Street, high‑energy mid‑set (Hell in a Bucket → Sugaree → Estimated Prophet), followed by an expansive 25‑minute post‑Space jam that builds to a satisfying encore.

band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively (“dang, Phil”) during Sugaree and noted teaching his son backstage, implying strong presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Referred to as “Bobby” going all‑out on Hell in a Bucket, indicating an energetic rhythm‑guitar performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent highlighted for beautiful vocal on “I Will Take You Home” and involvement in the chaotic Space/Drums section.
band_member_comments.Drums: Space/Drums described as especially chaotic, reflecting a wild but engaging percussive stretch.
